shared_properties: Exit gate keeps the 32x32 px gate body and warm gold highlight called out in the GDD.
Base stone trim uses the dungeon palette so the goal prop reads as part of the same prison architecture.
differences: This sheet adds a six-frame pulse cycle for the escape light while preserving a fixed gate silhouette for collision and interaction.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Exit gate size, six-frame pulse, and clear trigger grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Gold light bloom against dark stone grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down S3 GDD
reference_images.purpose: Goal prop placement in the lowest layer of the dungeon grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout for the six-frame gate pulse and spawn it as a goal SpriteBundle with a looping AnimationTimer.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/exit_gate_altar_sheet.png through AssetServer and map the clip to an ExitGateVisual component keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Pulse loop runs at 6 FPS and never changes the collision bounds.
Anchor the gate to the tile grid center at the final room floor.

context.role: Animated escape target that marks the bottom-most clear room.
context.usage: Visible only in the clear room and during the clear transition before return to title.

deliverables.notes: Six 32x32 frames for the gate pulse loop.
Clip map for the idle_pulse loop used by the clear-room goal entity.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 192x32 px with six 32x32 frames.
Pulse brightness escalates toward the center frames while the gate silhouette stays fixed in all six frames.
Gold highlight remains readable against the abyss background at 4x nearest-neighbor scaling.
